Details: Chlorine was not particularly effective for the removal of alachlor (30 percent) in bench-scale and full-scale studies with surface water. Sequential oxidation by chlorine following conventional treatment did not result in any greater increased in removal compared to chlorination only.  Higher removal percentages (up to 80 percent) were observed when chlorine was used in following conventional treatment and PAC addition.  The increased removal is predominantly attributed to the addition of PAC.
